cv_subtitle: Frontend Developer  ·  MSc Computer Science Candidate
profile: Frontend developer with 2+ years building production applications — a real-time parking management platform and a live banking dashboard used by external partner banks. Currently completing an MSc in Computer Science at the University of Lincoln (Distinction). Interested in understanding how things work under the hood: JavaScript engine behaviour, browser rendering, and HTTP — and applying that to write reliable, performant UI. Builds with React and modern tooling; comfortable owning work from design through deployment.

responsibilities: Built the operator dashboard for a B2B parking management platform — enabling apartment and commercial clients to monitor space availability, manage automated ticket machines, and review transaction history across sites. Structured the UI using React Router for navigation and hl{Atomic Design} principles for component organisation.
Integrated the frontend with hardware APIs for automated entry and exit machines, handling real-time polling and async state to keep the UI synchronised with physical machine events.
Added test coverage with Jest and Vitest to previously untested core modules, establishing a regression baseline and catching integration issues before deployment.
Contributed to a micro-frontend architecture enabling independent deployments across separate product areas.
Built cross-platform mobile views using React Native for on-site operators, and implemented multi-language support via React-i18next.

projects.name: VETC Smart Parking
projects.description: B2B parking management platform serving multiple partner sites
responsibilities: Built the web dashboard enabling multiple B2B partners (apartment complexes, commercial sites) to monitor parking space availability, manage automated entry/exit machines, and review transaction history across sites.
Developed a companion React Native mobile app for on-site operators, providing real-time access to gate status and transaction management from the parking floor.
Implemented multi-language support via React-i18next to serve operator teams across different partner locations.
Built and maintained components for a hl{production banking platform} used by external partner banks — working across transaction dashboards, data entry forms, and real-time financial data views.

Developed reusable React components following hl{Atomic Design} principles — structuring UI from atoms through to page-level compositions — to maintain consistency and long-term maintainability across the banking platform.
Integrated frontend with RESTful APIs, collaborating with backend teams to define interface contracts and maintain data consistency.
Maintained cross-browser compatibility and performance in a high-reliability, customer-facing codebase.

projects.name: KienLong Bank CMS
projects.description: Internal transaction management platform for KienLong Bank operators
responsibilities: Built and maintained the operator-facing frontend for KienLong Bank's transaction CMS — enabling bank staff to view, monitor, and manage client transactions across the platform.
Monitored live system behaviour, diagnosed frontend errors, and applied fixes to maintain uninterrupted workflows for bank operators.
Reported and escalated operational incidents to relevant departments, coordinating cross-team resolution of frontend issues.
Built a companion employee management portal for KienLong Bank, supporting internal staff administration alongside the core CMS.

personal_projects.name: L.I.V.E
personal_projects.subtitle: Learn. Improve. Visualise. Empower
personal_projects.description: Full-stack web application for gamified diabetes education — MSc Computer Science project, University of Lincoln. Sole owner from idea to production.
responsibilities: Took full ownership from idea to production: designed the architecture with hl{Atomic Design} component structure, built the frontend (React/Vite/Tailwind/shadcn) and backend (FastAPI/PostgreSQL), and deployed to Microsoft Azure.
Automated the build and deployment pipeline using Docker and Azure CI/CD, enabling reliable and repeatable deployments from day one.
Implemented secure authentication, guest access, and usage analytics.
Delivered a tested, working product on schedule as a solo project across the full stack.
